author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this Psalm, we ought to seek in the whole body of it what we find in the first verse: “Mercy and judgment will I sing unto Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man flatter himself that he will never be punished through God’s mercy; for there is judgment also; and let no man who hath been changed for the better dread the Lord’s judgment, seeing that mercy goeth before it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when men judge, sometimes overcome by mercy, they act against justice; and mercy, but not justice, seemeth to be in them: while sometimes, when they wish to enforce a rigid judgment, they lose mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God neither loseth the severity of judgment in the bounty of mercy, nor in judging with severity loseth the bounty of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose we distinguish these two, mercy and judgment, by time; for possibly, they are not placed in this order without a meaning, so that he said not “judgment and mercy,” but “mercy and judgment:” so that if we distinguish them by succession in time, perhaps we find that the present is the season for mercy, the future for judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is it that the season of mercy cometh first?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider first how it is with God, that thou also mayest imitate the Father, in so far as He shall permit thee.…“He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou seest the just and the unjust behold the same sun, enjoy the same light, drink from the same founts, satisfied with the same rain, blessed with the same fruits of the earth, inhale this air in the same way, possess equally the world’s goods; think not that God is unjust, who giveth these things equally to the just and the unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the season of mercy, not as yet of judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless God spared at first through mercy, He would not find those whom He could crown through judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is therefore a season for mercy, when the long-suffering of God calleth sinners to repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle distinguishing each season, and do thou also distinguish it.…“Thinkest thou,” he saith, “O man, that judgest them that do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the judgment of God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if we were to reply, Why do I commit such sins daily, and no evil occurreth unto me? he goeth on to show to him the season of mercy: “Despisest thou the riches of His goodness, and forbearance, and long-suffering?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he did indeed despise them; but the Apostle hath made him anxious.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Not knowing,” he saith, “that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the season of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that he might not think this would last for ever, how did he in the next verse raise his fears?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now hear the season of judgment; thou hast heard the season of mercy, on which account, “mercy and judgment will I sing unto Thee, O Lord:” “But thou,” saith the Apostle, “after thy hardness and impenitent heart, treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath, and revelation of the righteous judgment of God, who will render to every man according to his deeds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, “mercy and judgment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he hath threatened concerning judgment: is therefore the judgment of God to be feared only, and not to be loved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be feared by the wicked on account of punishment, to be loved by the good on account of the crown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then the Apostle hath alarmed the wicked in the testimony which I have quoted, hear where he giveth hope concerning judgment to the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He puts forth himself, and shows in himself too the season of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless he found a period of mercy, in what condition would judgment find him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A blasphemer, a persecutor, an injurer of others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he thus speaketh, and praiseth the season of mercy, in which season we are now living: “I who was before,” he saith, “a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps he only hath obtained mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear how he cheereth us: “That in me,” he saith, “first, Christ Jesus might show forth all long-suffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on Him to life everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “that He might show forth all long-suffering”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That every sinner and wicked man might see that Paul received pardon, and might not despair of himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, he hath instanced himself, and thereby cheered others also.…But did Paul alone deserve this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I had asserted, that as he raised our fears by the former testimony, so did he encourage us by the latter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he said, “The Lord, the righteous Judge, shall render to me at that day:” he addeth, “and not to me only, but unto all them also that love His appearing” and His kingdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore, brethren, we have a season of mercy, let us not on that account flatter, or indulge ourselves, saying, God spareth ever.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will sing to the harp, and will have understanding, in the spotless way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When Thou shalt come unto me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Except in the spotless way, thou canst neither sing to the harp, nor understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou dost wish to understand, sing in the spotless way, that is, work with cheerfulness before thy God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the spotless way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth: “I walked in innocence, in the midst of my house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This spotless way beginneth from innocence, and it endeth also in innocence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why seek many words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be innocent: and thou hast perfected righteousness.…But who is innocent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who while he hurteth not another, injureth not himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he who hurteth himself, is not innocent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some one saith: Lo, I have not robbed any one, I have not oppressed any one: I will live happily on my own substance, the fruits of my virtuous toil; I wish to have fine banquets, I wish to spend as much as pleaseth me, to drink with those whom I like as much as I please; whom have I robbed, whom have I oppressed, who hath complained of me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seemeth innocent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if he corrupt himself, if he overthrow the temple of God within himself, why hope that he will act with mercy toward others, and spare the wretched?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can that man be merciful to others, who unto himself is cruel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole of righteousness, therefore, is reduced to the one word, innocence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the lover of iniquity, hateth his own soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he loved iniquity, he fancied he was injuring others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider whether he was injuring others: “He who loveth iniquity,” he saith, “hateth his own soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who wishes to injure another, first injureth himself; nor doth he walk, since there is no room.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all wickedness suffereth from narrowness: innocence alone is broad enough to walk in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I walked in the innocence of my heart, in the midst of my house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the middle of his house, he either signifieth the Church herself; for Christ walketh in her: or his own heart; for our inner house is our heart: as he hath explained in the above words, “in the innocence of my heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the innocence of the heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The middle of his house?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever hath a bad house in this, is driven out of doors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whoever is oppressed within his heart by a bad conscience, just as any man in consequence of the overflow of a waterspout or of smoke goeth out of his house, suffereth not himself to dwell therein; so he who hath not a quiet heart, cannot happily dwell in his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such men go out of themselves in the bent of their mind, and delight themselves with things without, that affect the body; they seek repose in trifles, in spectacles, in luxuries, in all evils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore do they wish themselves well without?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is not well with them within, so that they may rejoice in a good conscience.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I set no wicked thing before my eyes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3).…I did love no wicked thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he explaineth this same wicked thing: “I hated them that do unfaithfulness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye walk with Christ in the midst of His house, that is, if either in your heart ye have a good repose, or in the Church herself proceed on a good journey in the way of godliness; ye ought not to hate those unfaithful only who are without, but whomsoever also ye may have found within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the unfaithful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who hate the law of God; who hear, and do it not, are called unfaithful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hate the doers of unfaithfulness, repel them from thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou shouldest hate the unfaithful, not men: one man who is unfaithful, hath, ye see, two names, man, and unfaithful: God made him man, he made himself unfaithful; love in him what God made, persecute in him what he made himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou shalt have persecuted his unfaithfulness, thou killest the work of man, and freest the work of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I hated the doers of unfaithfulness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The wicked heart hath not cleaved unto me.”…The heart of a man, who wisheth not anything contrary to any that God wisheth, is called straight.…If therefore the righteous heart followeth God, the crooked heart resisteth God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose something untoward happeneth to him, he crieth out, “God, what have I done unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sin have I committed?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He wisheth himself to appear just, God unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is so crooked as this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not enough that thou art crooked thyself: thou must think thy rule crooked also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reform thyself, and thou findest Him straight, in departing from whom thou hast made thyself crooked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth justly, thou unjustly; and for this reason thou art perverse, since thou callest man just, and God unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What man dost thou call just?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou sayest, “What have I done unto Thee?” thou thinkest thyself just.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let God answer thee: “Thou speakest truth: thou hast done nothing to Me: thou hast done all things unto thyself; for if thou hadst done anything for Me, thou wouldest have done good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whatever is done well, is done unto Me; because it is done according to My commandment; but whatever of evil is done, is done unto thee, not unto Me; for the wicked man doth nothing except for his own sake, since it is not what I command.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When ye see such men, brethren, reprove them, convince and correct them: and if ye cannot reprove or correct them, consent not to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the wicked man departed from me, I knew him not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I approved him not, I praised him not, he pleased me not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we find the word “to know” occasionally used in Scripture, in the sense of “to be pleased.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is hidden from God, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth He know the just, and doth He not know the unjust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou think of, that He doth not know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I say not, what thinkest thou; but what wilt thou ever think, that He will not have seen beforehand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God knoweth all things, then; and yet in the end, that is in judgment after mercy, He saith of some persons: “I will profess unto them, I never knew you; depart from Me, ye workers of iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was there any one He did not know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “I never knew you”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I acknowledge you not in My rule.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I know the rule of My righteousness: ye agree not with it, ye have turned aside from it, ye are crooked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore He said here also: “When the wicked man departed from Me, I knew him not.”…Therefore, “when the wicked man departed from me,” that is, when the wicked man was unlike me, and was unwilling to imitate my paths, was unwilling in his wickedness to live as I had proposed myself for his imitation; “I knew him not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “I knew him not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that I was ignorant of him, but that I did not approve him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whoso privily slandered his neighbour, him I persecuted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the righteous persecutor, not of the man, but of the sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With the proud eye, and the insatiable heart, I did not feed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “I did not feed with”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I did not eat in common with such.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, beloved; since ye are about to hear something wonderful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he did not feed with this man, he did not eat with him; for to feed is to eat; how is it then that we find our Lord Himself eating with the proud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was not only with those publicans and sinners, for they were humble: for they acknowledged their weakness, and asked for the physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We find that He ate with the proud Pharisees themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A certain proud man had invited Him: it was the same who was displeased because a sinning woman, one of ill repute in the city, approached the feet of our Lord.…That Pharisee was proud: the Lord ate with him; what is it therefore that he saith?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With such an one I did not eat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He enjoin unto us what He hath not done Himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He exhorteth us to imitate Himself: we see that He ate with the proud; how doth He forbid us to eat with the proud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We indeed, brethren, for the sake of reproof, abstain from communion with our brethren, and do not eat with them, that they may be reformed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We rather eat with strangers, with Pagans, than with those who hold with us, if we have seen that they live wickedly, that they may be ashamed, and amend; as the Apostle saith, “And if any man obey not our word by this Epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sake of healing others we usually do this; but nevertheless we often eat with many strangers and ungodly men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The pious heart hath its banquets, the proud heart hath its banquets: for it was on account of the food of the proud heart, that he said, “with an insatiable heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is the proud heart fed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a man is proud, he is envious: otherwise it cannot be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pride is the mother of enviousness: it cannot but generate it, and ever coexist with it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every proud man is, therefore, envious: if envious, he feedeth on the misfortunes of others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Apostle saith, “But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed of one another.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see them, then, eating: eat not with these: fly such banquets: for they cannot satisfy themselves with rejoicing in others’ evils, because their hearts are insatiable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beware thou art not caught in their feasts by the devil’s noose.…Just as birds feed at the trap, or fishes at the hook, they were taken, when they fed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ungodly therefore have their own feasts, the godly also have theirs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the feasts of the godly: “Blessed are they who hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore the godly eateth the meat of righteousness, and the ungodly of pride; it is no wonder if he is insatiable in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He eateth the meat of iniquity: do not eat the meat of iniquity, and the proud in eye, and the insatiable in heart, eateth not with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence wast thou fed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what pleased thee, when he did not eat with thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine eyes,” he saith, “were upon such as are faithful in the land, that they might sit with me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, that with Me they might be seated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what sense are they “to sit”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ye shall sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The faithful of the earth judge, for to them it is said, “Know ye not, that we shall judge angels?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whoso walketh in a spotless way, he ministered unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To “Me,” he saith, not to himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many minister the Gospel, but unto themselves; because they seek their own things, not the things of Jesus Christ.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The proud man hath not dwelt in the midst of my house” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand this of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The proud did not dwell in my heart: no such dwelt in my heart: for he hurried away from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: None but the meek and peaceful dwelt in my heart; the proud dwelt not there, for the unrighteous one dwelleth not in the heart of the righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the righteous be distant from thee, I know not how many miles and stations: ye dwell together, if ye have one heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The proud doer hath not dwelt in the midst of my house: he that speaketh unjust things hath not directed in the sight of my eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the spotless way, where we understand when the Lord cometh unto us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the morning I destroyed all the ungodly that were in the land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I may root out all wicked doers from the city of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is obscure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are then wicked doers in the city of the Lord, and they at present, seemingly, spared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is the season of mercy: but that of judgment will come; for the Psalm thus began, “Of mercy and judgment will I sing unto Thee, O Lord.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He at present spareth, He will then judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when will He judge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When night shall have passed away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason He hath said: “In the morning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the day shall at last have arrived, night having passed by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth He spare them until the dawn?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it was night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, it was night?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it was the season for mercy: He was merciful, while the hearts of men were hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest some one living ill; thou endurest him: for thou knowest not of what sort he will prove to be; since it is night; whether he who to-day liveth ill, to-morrow may live well; and whether he who to-day liveth well, to-morrow may be wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is night, and God endureth all men, since He is of long-suffering: He endureth them, that sinners may be converted unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who shall not have reformed themselves in that season of mercy, shall be slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they may be scattered abroad from the city of the Lord, from the fellowship of Jerusalem, from the fellowship of the Saints, from the fellowship of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when shall they be slain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “at dawn”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When night shall have passed away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore now doth he spare?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is the season of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth He not always spare?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “Mercy and judgment will I sing unto Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, let no man flatter himself: all the doers of iniquity shall be slain; Christ shall slay them at the dawn, and shall destroy them from His city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now while it is the time of mercy, let them hear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Everywhere He crieth out by the Law, by the Prophets, by the Psalms, by the Epistles, by the Gospels: see that He is not silent; that He spareth; that He granteth mercy; but beware, for the judgment will come.
